title: parietaria officinalis var. judaica
intro: Parietaria officinalis var. judaica, also known as Pellitory-of-the-wall, is a plant species within the Urticaceae family, characterized by its distinctive, heart-shaped leaves and small, greenish-yellow flowers. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing symptoms of rheumatism, arthritis, and eczema, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Parietaria officinalis var. judaica are often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes.
